title: HTML Tokens, Error Recovery, Parser Selection, and Differential Evidence · Code Recall
summary: A delayed crawl review reconstructs html tokens, error recovery, parser selection, and differential evidence from authorization, revision, seed, URL key, response identity, robots decision, parse record, extraction provenance, frontier event, report row, failure, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for bounded parser and tree-behavior comparison after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debu…
objectives: Distinguish source bytes, decoded text, tokens, parse errors, the constructed tree, serialized markup, and browser DOM state.
Predict omitted tags, mismatched nesting, duplicate attributes, character references, templates, foreign content, and recovery effects on target nodes.
Select html.parser, lxml HTML, lxml XML, or html5lib deliberately and pin that parser in code, tests, and metadata.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

stimulus.title: Parser Differential case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review site-quality team handles case 5615 during a local-server failure rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate malformed HTML recovery, explicit parser identity, script boundaries, and differential tree evidence; the target owner must be able to pause the crawl; then change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare parser trees on adversarial fixtures and require extraction contracts to state which differences are acceptable.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Parser differential tests are unnecessary once the happy fixture passes.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare parser trees on adversarial fixtures and require extraction contracts to state which differences are acceptable. Artifact under review: bounded parser and tree-behavior comparison.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Parser Differential, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Record what required effort · Review Parser Differential
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Parser Differential: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review site-quality team handles case 7234 during an authorized nonprofit content audit. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate malformed HTML recovery, explicit parser identity, script boundaries, and differential tree evidence; the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure; then introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare parser trees on adversarial fixtures and require extraction contracts to state which differences are acceptable.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Record what required effort · Review Parser Differential.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Authorized crawling and Python Record what required effort · Review Parser Differential, keep Parser Differential connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare parser trees on adversarial fixtures and require extraction contracts to state which differences are acceptable.
